header.stance: 損益兩平通膨率帶動美債壓力緩解，但實質殖利率仍具限制性，供給風險亦未消退

zh-TW.long_thesis_markdown:
## 固定收益開盤主論點

6月4日美國公債收盤扭轉了前一交易日的方向，但並未推翻其核心政策訊息。整條殖利率曲線的名目殖利率溫和下滑，並由前端領漲，形成小幅牛市趨陡。此波走勢集中於 10 年期通膨補償下降，而相應的實質殖利率則維持在 6月3日所觸及的高檔。投資人因此下修了近期通膨動能，卻未接受聯準會政策路徑將顯著轉向寬鬆的看法。

3項管道塑造了此一結果。初領失業救濟人數升至 4 個月高點，修正後的生產力數據則顯示產出效率較弱，但單位勞動成本增幅較小。這些數據緩和了強韌需求與持續成本壓力並存的局面，而該組合正是前一日實質殖利率遭拋售的原因。原油亦大幅回落，緩解近期最明顯的通膨壓力來源。然而，Fed 溝通仍刻意維持雙向立場；由於核心通膨、財政支持、信用成長及 AI 相關投資仍使名目需求保持強勁，期貨市場繼續賦予年底前升息相當高的機率。

公私部門融資環境使本波漲勢維持有序。2場國庫券標售的投標倍數均高於前次，美國財政部完成全額短期附息債券買回，上市信用債報酬亦為正。公司進入市場的管道仍夠寬廣，使 Schneider Electric 得以啟動與其 AI 基礎設施曝險相關的可轉債再融資。與此同時，Reno de Medici的初步債轉股重整，以及菸草和解市政債領域第1次未能償付本金，顯示充裕資本仍是以選擇性方式配置。

本次收盤最適合被理解為限制性利率環境中的有條件壓力緩解。油價下跌、勞動成本壓力減輕及初領失業救濟人數增加，足以支持當日較低的名目期限結構。然而，實質殖利率居高不下、已公布的大規模美國財政部發行日程、公司債存續期間供給增加，以及房貸凸性避險風險再起，使這波壓力緩解無法轉化為明確的存續期間突破行情。前一交易日的價格跌勢獲得部分扭轉，但其對資本成本發出的警訊仍然有效。

## 美國公債利率、Fed 定價、實質殖利率與殖利率曲線結構

美國公債殖利率下降 2 至 3 個基點。2 年期收於 4.05%，5 年期收於 4.18%，兩者均下跌 3 個基點；10 年期收於 4.47%，30 年期收於 4.97%，兩者均下跌 2 個基點。2s10s 殖利率曲線趨陡 1 個基點至 42 個基點，2s30s 亦趨陡 1 個基點至 92。前端略為領漲，符合勞動市場訊號轉弱、降低近期政策壓力的盤勢；不過，本波漲幅有限，仍保留了此前 1 個月所累積的整體趨平走勢。

通膨連結定價更精確地指出名目殖利率變動的來源。10 年期實質殖利率持平於 2.11%，10 年期損益兩平通膨率則下降 2 個基點至 2.36%，完全解釋了名目 10 年期殖利率的降幅。5 年期與 7 年期實質殖利率分別上升 2 個及 1 個基點，至 1.70% 與 1.90%。20 年期與 30 年期實質殖利率各下降 1 個基點，至 2.50% 與 2.71%。市場降低了指標年期附近的通膨補償，但仍在殖利率曲線中段保留相當大的實質政策壓力。

08:30 ET 公布的數據支持此一分化。截至 5月30日當週，初領失業救濟人數增加 13,000 至 225,000，高於 FactSet 共識預期的 211,000，前值則下修至 212,000。4 週平均由修正後的 208,250 升至 214,750。第 1 季非農業生產力年化增幅由 0.8% 下修至 0.3%，低於 0.7%的共識預期；然而，單位勞動成本增幅由 2.3% 下修至 1.8%。勞動成本壓力放緩有利於損益兩平通膨率下降；生產力較弱則限制了實質殖利率的緩解幅度。

儘管美債上漲，政策定價仍呈不對稱。聯邦基金期貨隱含年底前收緊政策的機率約為 2/3。Morgan Stanley的 Andrew Sheets 認為，Fed 更可能將伊朗戰爭視為成長衝擊，而非任何升息的理由，並將政策風險歸因於核心通膨、財政支持、銀行放款及 AI 支出。舊金山聯準銀行總裁 Mary Daly 表示，目前政策處於良好位置，但強調已準備好因應任何方向的變化。在 4月 PCE 通膨為 3.8%、失業率為 4.3%的情況下，初領失業救濟人數轉弱只改變了邊際判斷，並未改變反應函數。

國庫券殖利率曲線未顯示普遍性的現金緊縮。4 週期票息等值殖利率持平於 3.68%，6 週期殖利率下降 1 個基點至 3.68%，8 週期則持穩於 3.69%。13 週期與 26 週期分別持平於 3.71% 及 3.77%，52 週期殖利率則下降 2 個基點至 3.80%。如此狹窄的差異，將未來 Fed 政策的爭論與眼前前端融資環境區隔開來。

已完成的標售進一步強化了承接訊號。美國財政部接受了 $750.02億的 28 天期國庫券，最高利率為 3.615%，投標倍數為 3.07，高於前次的 2.76；間接投標人承接 51.60%，交易商承接 45.99%。$750.01億的 56 天期標售以 3.610% 得標，投標倍數為 3.20，高於前次標售的 3.08；間接需求達 65.06%，交易商承接比例為 31.92%。2場標售都顯示短期供給獲得充足投標，其中 8 週期標售的非交易商需求組合更為理想。

美國財政部的公告將焦點由國庫券轉向下一次存續期間考驗。該部敲定發行 $580億的新 3 年期公債、增額發行 $390億的 10 年期公債及 $220億的 30 年期公債，使附息債券發行序列總額維持在 $1,190億，規模與前一月週期相同。另宣布發行合計 $2,810億的 6 週期、13 週期、26 週期及 52 週期國庫券。唯一縮減的是 6 週期發行，規模由 $750億降至 $650億。這些公告確立了未來供給，卻尚未證明標售需求。

現金管理在短期附息債券端反向運作。美國財政部在 1 個月至 2 年期債券買回中，從投標的 $416.62億接受全部 $125億上限，共納入 49 檔合格券中的 21 檔。此操作吸收了流通中的短期附息債券並支援流動性，但既未改變貨幣政策，也未解決更龐大的淨供給負擔。其規模配合穩健的國庫券投標，有助解釋為何收盤時未見明顯融資壓力。

在這個原本受控的盤勢中，房貸避險仍是放大因素。根據 Barclays，超過 $2兆的房貸證券目前票息為 5% 或以上，約 1/3的市場價格接近面額，而該區域對再融資最為敏感。殖利率上升時，延長風險可能促使投資人賣出美國公債存續期間；市場上漲時則可能引發反向資金流。此機制並未被認定為 6月4日的主導力量，但其影響範圍擴大，提高了未來殖利率一旦突破時，波幅超越最初總體衝擊的可能性。

## 信用、融資與初級市場承接能力

信用債總報酬隨美國公債上漲及風險環境趨穩而改善。LQD 上漲 0.2117%，HYG 上漲 0.1883%，JNK 上漲 0.1563%。這些工具結合了政府利率存續期間、票息收益及信用曝險，因此發行執行情況與重整條款更能有效反映市場准入。整體訊息是融資功能正常，但企業品質、擔保品及資本結構順位之間存在明顯差異。

新融資規模本身正逐漸成為存續期間議題。美國投資級債券發行量以 2020 年以來最快速度達到 $1兆，部分由 AI 投資所推動。Apollo 總裁 Jim Zelter 表示，投資級債券發行量今年可能超過美國財政部淨發行量，並提及本會計年度美國財政部新增現金融資約為 $1.4兆。他的預測並非已完成的資金流結果，但確實捕捉到科技、公用事業、國防及政府借款在長期資本上的競爭。

Schneider Electric 顯示，強勁的股票表現如何拓寬融資選項。該公司發行 €8億、2034 到期的可轉債，建議票息為 0.25%-0.75%，轉換溢價為 35%-40%。公司計畫動用 €6.5億買回 11月 2030 到期的可轉債，其餘則供一般用途。Schneider 股價已升越舊債的轉換價格，因此再融資可在處理股權稀釋風險的同時，讓投資人繼續參與資料中心供電與冷卻需求。

困境信用債呈現更嚴峻的負債管理形式。Apollo、Reno de Medici 與這家包裝公司 €6億債券的持有人達成初步協議，債券持有人將部分債權轉換為多數股權，並提供新資金。協商仍須納入 €1.45億循環融資的貸款人。由於債券報價接近每 1 歐元面額 26 歐分，且自由現金流預計到 2026 年仍為負值，控制權移轉及優先流動性正取代傳統再融資。

市政債市場則出現另一個特殊損失案例。Nassau County的1家機構未能於 6月1日償付 $3,600萬本金，成為規模約 $800億的菸草和解債券市場第1次違約。這些證券依賴與香菸銷售掛鉤的付款，而 2025 年向參與州及領地的分配金額下降 19%。2046 到期債券於 6月2日的成交價為 58.5 美分。此結構會將未付本金展延至未來和解收入，因此回收價值取決於1個持續萎縮的現金流基礎。

綜合而言，這些案例勾勒出1個分割的市場。投資級及股票連結型借款人可利用有利估值進行再融資並支應擴張；營運現金流疲弱者則迫使債權人取得所有權並提供新資金支援。上市信用債報酬為正、美國財政部現金操作順利，顯示流動性並未全面撤離。這些情況與個別證券減損並存，同時公私部門存續期間供給增加，日後可能需要提供更高補償。

## 全球存續期間、央行與財政傳導

在通膨爭論仍然棘手之際，歐洲成長數據為存續期間資產提供支撐。歐元區 4月零售量下降 0.4%，3月增幅則修正為 0.8%，表現略弱於預期的下降 0.3%。銷售量較 1 年前增加 1.0%，但非食品銷量當月下降 0.9%，汽車燃料則下降 2.7%。數據顯示商品需求受抑，削弱了每次能源帶動的價格上漲都必須以持續收緊政策因應的論點。

瑞典顯示各國殖利率曲線為何不必同步變動。核心 CPIF 通膨由 5月的 0% 升至 0.5%，高於調查中位數的 0.3%，但遠低於瑞典央行預測的 0.9%。整體 CPIF 由 0.8% 升至 1.5%，接近央行預測，瑞典克朗亦溫和走強。食品及燃料方面的財政減負抑制了成本轉嫁，使 1.75%的政策利率仍可作為可信的近期錨點，即使市場仍保留部分升息溢價。

捷克通膨亦緩和了區域內的鷹派動能。5月整體物價較 1 年前上漲 2.1%，低於 2.3%的預估中位數，並符合央行預測。服務通膨放緩至 4.7%，但仍高到足以使第2輪效應成為焦點。在政策利率為 3.5%的情況下，此一組合削弱立即升息的理由，同時仍對能源成本再次轉嫁至基礎物價保持敏感。

紐西蘭顯示，供給稀缺可獨立於政策預期而發揮力量。政府下修預計借款後，NZ$2.25億的 2031 政府公債吸引 NZ$14億投標，使該債券的投標倍數創下 6.22的紀錄。自預算公布以來，指標 5 年期殖利率已下降超過 5 個基點，即使投資人仍預期央行最終將收緊政策。發行減少及高絕對票息吸引需求，使主權供給表現與政策利率展望脫鉤。

印度仍是能源及匯率壓力由已開發市場傳導至新興市場最明顯的管道。盧比今年已貶值 6%，曾逼近 1 美元兌 97 盧比，即使主權債殖利率本季大致持穩。據了解 Reliance Industries 財務部門討論的人士稱，公司可能轉向較短期的貨幣市場票券並降低長存續期間曝險；該公司否認曾表達正式的利率或匯率觀點。此事件仍說明，在主權融資本身失序之前，石油進口成本及匯率防衛如何先縮短民間部門對存續期間的需求。

## 新興市場主權債、美元流動性與債務管理風險

新興市場債券參與了較平穩的收盤走勢，美元轉弱之際 EMB 上漲 0.2085%。整體報酬掩蓋了截然不同的主權機制。商品曝險及殖利率支撐剛果民主共和國；合約門檻主導尚比亞；新增借款則考驗智利的財政可信度。外部流動性仍可取得，但其價格取決於各借款人的收入基礎及債務管理選擇。

剛果的 2032 與 2037 美元債連續第 3 個交易日上漲，價格維持高於面額，殖利率分別為 8.3% 及 9.03%。投資人淡化集中於衝突地區的 Ebola 疫情，轉而關注銅、鈷對電力、電氣化及 AI 基礎設施的曝險。該主權國於 4月首次進入市場時籌得 $12.5億。高票息及戰略礦產收入支撐債券；若生產或出口受阻，仍是這場衛生危機可能衝擊主權現金流的管道。

尚比亞的 2053 債券仍是1場圍繞回收價值與合約機制的協商。Morgan Stanley 預期政府可能將提前買回價格由 78 美分提高至約 85 美分，而該債券成交價為 82.14 美分。啟動清理贖回條款需要接近 75%的接受率，但1個阻擋集團控制超過 25%的債券。若符合 IMF 債務可持續性條件，票息將由 0.5% 或有提高至 7.5%，形成政府註銷該債券的誘因。提高報價可能增加參與率，卻會降低政府的經濟利益。

智利則從較強的信用地位面臨傳統供給問題。政府尋求授權額外發行 $62億，增列於 2026 年已獲准的 $174億之上。2032 到期的披索債券以較次級市場殖利率高 12 個基點完成發行，後續 2056 債券標售則須提供 10 個基點讓利。3月的政府總債務為 GDP的 42.6%，預計到 2028 年將超越政府設定的 45% 審慎門檻。智利仍擁有拉丁美洲最高的主權評等，但本地供給增加或額外美元借款，將使財政鬆動傳導至殖利率與利差。

這種分化顯示，不應將基金正報酬視為信用狀況全面改善。剛果因稀缺商品曝險及實質資金買盤而獲得獎勵。尚比亞的結果取決於參與率、要約經濟效益及票息調升條款。智利的問題則是財政整頓能否伴隨新增市場融資。美元當日轉弱，緩解了共同的外部限制，使各國特有的現金流、制度及負債條款決定邊際定價。

## 跨資產

原油為美債漲勢提供了最強的跨資產支撐。WTI 下跌 3.1035% 至 $93.04，Brent 下跌 2.8422% 至 $95.03。此次回落與 10 年期損益兩平通膨率下降 2 個基點相符，並有助扭轉前一交易日的通膨動能。油價仍高到足以延續市場對商品、運輸及肥料成本的擔憂，因此本波走勢降低了近期壓力，卻未解決更廣泛的成本轉嫁風險。

股票及波動率否定了將殖利率下降解讀為衰退訊號的看法。S&P 500 上漲 0.4055%，Nasdaq 100 則下跌 0.5346%，半導體指數下跌 2.1518%。VIX 下跌 4.1096% 至 15.4。整體風險偏好維持，但 AI 敏感型成長股疲弱，使原本支持公司債發行及實質需求的資本支出敘事更為複雜。因此，利率上漲行情伴隨的是類股輪動，而非全面性的避險潮。

美元指數下跌 0.1206% 至 99.41，緩解新興市場借款人的外部融資環境。黃金上漲 0.8813% 至 $4,475.7998，即使波動率下降，市場仍保留部分對地緣政治及通膨避險的需求。信用債基金正報酬完成了1個有序格局：油價下跌及美元轉弱強化名目美債漲勢，而黃金堅挺、實質殖利率居高不下及科技股表現分歧，使整體環境尚未顯得完全正常化。

## 市場前瞻意義

6月5日 08:30 ET 公布的 5月就業報告，接續 6月10日 08:30 ET的 5月 CPI，以及 6月11日 08:30 ET的 PPI 與每週初領失業救濟人數，將決定 6月4日由損益兩平通膨率帶動的漲勢，能否擴展至實質殖利率下降。非農就業成長接近 85,000的共識預期、失業率維持 4.3%，且薪資上漲廣度受控，將維持目前的解讀：勞動市場正逐步降溫，但幅度尚不足以排除 Fed的緊縮選項。若就業組合明顯轉弱，隨後核心通膨亦走軟，將挑戰具限制性的實質利率底部，並帶動 2 年期與 5 年期區段殖利率下滑。招聘強勁或核心與上游通膨更為廣泛，則會重新開啟 6月3日的賣壓，前端期貨、5 至 10 年期實質殖利率及損益兩平通膨率將透過不同管道反應。

印度央行於 6月5日的決策及歐洲 Central Bank 於 6月11日的決策，將在能源進口型經濟體衡量通膨與需求轉弱之際，構成全球政策考驗。印度央行維持 5.25% 利率並搭配穩定盧比的措施，加上 ECB 指引限制任何物價漲幅的持續性，將證實在原油回落時，各國反應函數仍可維持分化。油價再度上漲、進口國貨幣轉弱，或政策指引強調第2輪價格壓力，將挑戰此一壓力緩解，並推升當地前端利率、全球期限溢價競爭及美元融資負擔。瑞典與捷克通膨顯示成本轉嫁並不同步；下一波政策措辭將揭示此種分化能否經受能源價格再次變動。

美國財政部的 6月8日至9日國庫券標售及 6月9日至11日附息債券發行序列，將檢驗 6月4日所見的順暢短端承接能否延伸至存續期間。$890億的 13 週期與 $770億的 26 週期國庫券於 6月11日交割，另有 $650億的 6 週期與 $500億的 52 週期供給；$580億的 3 年期、$390億的 10 年期及 $220億的 30 年期公債則於 6月15日交割。投標倍數強勁、直接與間接投標人參與廣泛、交易商承接比例有限且前端殖利率穩定，將確認總體重新定價仍與融資壓力有所區隔。非農就業及 CPI 公布後，若附息債券需求疲弱，將挑戰此一觀點，透過供給讓利使殖利率曲線趨陡，並在公司借款人競逐相同存續期間承接能力之際，提高房貸凸性避險放大走勢的可能性。
zh-TW.methodology_note: 除非另有註明，市場水準均以紐約收盤為基準。

overall_summary.headline: 油價下跌與勞動市場訊號轉弱推動美債溫和上漲，但實質殖利率仍維持堅挺
overall_summary.session_narrative: 初領失業救濟人數上升、單位勞動成本下修及原油回落，降低近期通膨壓力，帶動美債殖利率溫和下滑，並由前端領跌。指標名目殖利率的降幅來自損益兩平通膨率下降，而非實質殖利率走低；信用債報酬及國庫券需求則維持有序。政策選擇空間仍大、大規模附息債券發行日程將至，加上房貸凸性風險，共同限制了本波走勢。
overall_summary.summary: 截至 6月4日紐約收盤，前端表現領先、通膨補償下降，帶動殖利率曲線小幅牛市趨陡。勞動成本壓力減輕及原油回落，緩和了近期通膨動能；然而，實質殖利率居高不下，加上存續期間供給將至，限制性利率環境仍未改變。

section_summaries.market_read: 本波上漲緩解了名目利率壓力，但未撼動具限制性的實質利率環境。
section_summaries.next_check: 較疲弱的總體數據能否最終帶動實質殖利率下滑，仍是決定性考驗。
section_summaries.summary: 6月4日收盤走勢透過通膨補償下降，扭轉了前一日部分賣壓，但實質殖利率仍處高檔。有序的融資環境協助控制市場波動；然而，Fed 政策選擇空間、即將到來的公債與公司債供給，以及房貸避險風險，意味這只是有條件的壓力緩解，而非存續期間資產的突破行情。

section_summaries.market_read: 政策重新定價仍與融資壓力有所區隔，但存續期間供給尚未經受考驗。
section_summaries.next_check: 非農就業數據公布後，附息債券標售參與情況與實質殖利率表現，將檢驗此一分化。
section_summaries.section_title: 美國公債利率、Fed 定價、實質殖利率與殖利率曲線結構
section_summaries.summary: 2 年期與 5 年期殖利率領漲，帶動美債溫和上揚及殖利率曲線小幅牛市趨陡；但指標殖利率變動完全來自損益兩平通膨率下降，10 年期實質殖利率則持穩。國庫券需求強勁，顯示現金市場並未緊縮，下一項風險因而轉向附息債券承接能力與房貸避險。

section_summaries.market_read: 整體報酬有序，掩蓋了市場准入、回收價值及結構方面日益擴大的分化。
section_summaries.next_check: 新券讓利與困境融資條件，將顯示市場選擇性是否進一步升高。
section_summaries.section_title: 信用、融資與初級市場承接能力
section_summaries.summary: 信用債基金報酬為正，且投資級發行人普遍仍可進入市場，顯示流動性運作正常，但融資條件依然高度選擇性。與股票連結的發行人得以再融資並支應擴張；財務困難的借款人則面臨所有權移轉、新資金支援或付款受損，同時公債與公司債的存續期間供給也在競逐資本。

section_summaries.market_read: 全球存續期間走勢仍由各地供給與成本轉嫁主導，而非同步的政策轉向。
section_summaries.next_check: 央行指引將顯示，在能源壓力再起之際，政策分化能否延續。
section_summaries.section_title: 全球存續期間、央行與財政傳導
section_summaries.summary: 歐元區需求疲弱及捷克通膨轉弱支撐存續期間資產，瑞典的通膨組合則維持各國政策分化。紐西蘭供給稀少，即使市場預期政策將收緊，仍吸引極強需求；印度則顯示，油價及匯率壓力可在主權融資失序前，先縮短民間部門的存續期間偏好。

section_summaries.market_read: 美元壓力緩解了共同限制，但負債條款與財政選擇仍驅動市場分化。
section_summaries.next_check: 商品現金流、尚比亞的參與門檻及智利的融資讓利仍是決定性因素。
section_summaries.section_title: 新興市場主權債、美元流動性與債務管理風險
section_summaries.summary: 美元轉弱有利新興市場債券，但各國主權債定價仍具個別性，並非全面走強。剛果受惠於戰略礦產現金流；尚比亞取決於要約參與率及合約經濟效益；智利則因新增借款考驗財政可信度，而必須提供供給讓利。

section_summaries.market_read: 跨資產定價顯示，本波利率走勢是有序的通膨壓力緩解，而非成長恐慌。
section_summaries.next_check: 油價轉嫁與 AI 敏感型股票的表現，將檢驗此一平衡能否延續。
section_summaries.summary: 原油下跌及美元轉弱強化了名目美債漲勢，並緩解外部融資環境。大盤股票上漲且波動率下降，否定了衰退訊號；但科技股疲弱、黃金堅挺及實質殖利率居高不下，使跨資產環境尚未呈現完全正常化。

section_summaries.market_read: 下一波走勢取決於總體壓力緩解與存續期間供給承接能否同時出現。
section_summaries.next_check: 非農就業、通膨、政策指引及附息債券需求，必須共同確認相同的寬鬆脈絡。
section_summaries.summary: 3項考驗將決定由損益兩平通膨率帶動的漲勢能否擴大：美國勞動與通膨數據、印度央行與 ECB的政策指引，以及美國公債標售序列。數據轉弱、能源成本轉嫁受控及附息債券需求強勁，將延續壓力緩解；相反結果則會對實質殖利率、前端及殖利率曲線供給形成壓力。

watch_events.market_implication: 數據疲弱將帶動 2 年期及 5 年期殖利率下滑；數據強勁則會重新推升實質殖利率與損益兩平通膨率。
watch_events.market_trigger: 勞動市場明顯轉弱且核心通膨走軟，或招聘強勁且通膨廣泛升溫。
watch_events.title: 美國勞動與通膨數據序列
watch_events.watch_line: 檢視接近 85,000的非農就業、4.3% 失業率及受控薪資，並與通膨廣度相互對照。

watch_events.market_implication: 當地前端利率、全球期限溢價及美元融資負擔將上升。
watch_events.market_trigger: 油價再度上漲、進口國貨幣轉弱，或政策指引著重第2輪價格壓力。
watch_events.title: 印度央行與 ECB 政策考驗
watch_events.watch_line: 關注盧比支撐措施、ECB 對通膨持續性的指引，以及能源成本是否再度轉嫁。

watch_events.market_implication: 供給讓利將使殖利率曲線趨陡，並提高房貸避險的放大風險。
watch_events.market_trigger: 非農就業與 CPI 公布後附息債券需求疲弱，尤其是交易商承接比例較高時。
watch_events.title: 美國公債存續期間承接能力考驗
watch_events.watch_line: 追蹤整個標售序列的投標倍數、投標人廣度、交易商承接比例及前端穩定性。

long_thesis.english_markdown:
## Opening Fixed-Income Thesis

The June 4 Treasury close reversed the prior session’s direction without overturning its central policy message. Nominal yields fell modestly across the curve and the front end led the rally, producing a small bull steepening. The move was concentrated in lower inflation compensation at 10 years, while the corresponding real yield held at the elevated level reached on June 3. Investors therefore marked down the immediate inflation impulse without embracing a materially easier Federal Reserve path.

3 channels shaped that outcome. Initial claims reached a 4-month high, and revised productivity data paired weaker output efficiency with a smaller increase in unit labor costs. Those releases softened the combination of resilient demand and persistent costs that had driven the previous real-yield selloff. Crude also retreated sharply, relieving the most visible source of near-term inflation pressure. Yet Fed communication remained deliberately two-sided, and futures continued to assign substantial odds to a rate increase by year-end because core inflation, fiscal support, credit growth and AI-related investment still leave nominal demand firm.

Public and private financing conditions kept the rally orderly. 2 bill auctions drew stronger cover than their preceding sales, Treasury completed a full-size short-coupon buyback, and listed credit returns were positive. Corporate access remained broad enough for Schneider Electric to launch a convertible refinancing tied to its AI-infrastructure exposure. At the same time, Reno de Medici’s preliminary debt-for-equity restructuring and the 1st missed principal payment in the tobacco-settlement municipal sector showed that abundant capital was being allocated selectively.

The close is best understood as conditional relief inside a restrictive-rate regime. Lower oil, softer labor-cost pressure and higher claims justified a lower nominal term structure for the day. Sticky real yields, a large announced Treasury calendar, growing corporate duration supply and renewed mortgage-convexity hedging risk prevented that relief from becoming a clean duration breakout. The previous session’s selloff was partly reversed in price, while its warning about the cost of capital remained operative.

## UST Rates, Fed Pricing, Real Yields, And Curve Structure

Treasury yields declined by 2 to 3 basis points. The 2-year closed at 4.05% and the 5-year at 4.18%, each down 3 basis points; the 10-year ended at 4.47% and the 30-year at 4.97%, each down 2. The 2s10s curve steepened 1 basis point to 42 basis points, and 2s30s steepened 1 to 92. The front end’s slight outperformance fits a session in which softer labor signals reduced immediate policy pressure, though the modest scale of the rally preserved the broader flattening accumulated over the preceding month.

Inflation-linked pricing located the nominal move more precisely. The 10-year real yield was unchanged at 2.11%, while the 10-year breakeven fell 2 basis points to 2.36%, accounting for the nominal 10-year decline. 5- and 7-year real yields rose 2 and 1 basis points to 1.70% and 1.90%. 20- and 30-year real yields each fell 1 basis point, to 2.50% and 2.71%. The market reduced inflation compensation around the benchmark maturity but kept substantial real-policy pressure in the belly.

The 08:30 ET releases supported that split. Initial claims rose 13,000 to 225,000 in the week ended May 30, above the 211,000 FactSet consensus and the downwardly revised 212,000 prior reading. The 4-week average climbed to 214,750 from a revised 208,250. Nonfarm productivity was revised to a 0.3% annualized Q1 gain from 0.8%, below the 0.7% consensus, yet unit labor-cost growth was revised down to 1.8% from 2.3%. Slower labor-cost pressure favored lower breakevens; weaker productivity limited the relief for real yields.

Policy pricing remained asymmetric despite the rally. Federal-funds futures implied about a 2/3 probability of tightening by year-end. Morgan Stanley’s Andrew Sheets argued that the Fed was more likely to treat the Iran war as a growth shock than as the reason for any increase, locating the policy risk in core inflation, fiscal support, bank lending and AI spending. San Francisco Fed President Mary Daly described current policy as well placed but emphasized readiness to respond in either direction. With April PCE inflation at 3.8% and unemployment at 4.3%, softer claims altered the margin, not the reaction function.

The bill curve showed no generalized cash squeeze. 4-week coupon-equivalent yields were unchanged at 3.68%, 6-week yields fell 1 basis point to 3.68%, and 8-week yields held at 3.69%. The 13-week and 26-week tenors were unchanged at 3.71% and 3.77%, while the 52-week yield fell 2 basis points to 3.80%. That narrow dispersion separated the debate over future Fed policy from immediate front-end funding conditions.

Completed auctions reinforced the absorption signal. Treasury accepted $75.002 billion of 28-day bills at a 3.615% high rate with 3.07 cover, up from 2.76 previously; indirect bidders took 51.60% and dealers 45.99%. The $75.001 billion 56-day sale stopped at 3.610% with 3.20 cover, versus 3.08 at the prior auction, while indirect demand reached 65.06% and dealer takedown was 31.92%. Both results showed ample bids for shorter supply, with the 8-week sale drawing the cleaner nondealer mix.

Treasury’s announcements shifted attention from bills toward the next duration test. The department fixed $58 billion of new 3-year notes, a $39 billion 10-year reopening and a $22 billion 30-year reopening, leaving the total coupon sequence at $119 billion and the amounts unchanged from the previous monthly cycle. It also announced $281 billion across 6-, 13-, 26- and 52-week bills. The only size reduction was the 6-week offering, cut to $65 billion from $75 billion. The announcements established future supply; they did not establish auction demand.

Cash management operated in the opposite direction at the short-coupon end. Treasury accepted the full $12.5 billion cap in its 1-month-to-2-year buyback from $41.662 billion offered, taking 21 of 49 eligible issues. The operation absorbed seasoned short coupons and supported liquidity without changing monetary policy or resolving the larger net-supply burden. Its scale alongside firm bill bidding helped explain why the close showed no visible funding stress.

Mortgage hedging remains an amplifier around this otherwise controlled tape. More than $2 trillion of mortgage securities now carry coupons of 5% or higher, according to Barclays, and about 1/3 of the market is trading near par, where refinancing sensitivity is greatest. When yields rise, extension risk can prompt investors to sell Treasury duration; rallies can induce the reverse flow. The mechanism was not identified as the dominant force on June 4, but its growing footprint raises the chance that a future break in yields becomes larger than the initiating macro shock.

## Credit, Funding, And Primary-Market Absorption

Credit total returns improved with Treasuries and a calmer risk backdrop. LQD gained 0.2117%, HYG rose 0.1883%, and JNK advanced 0.1563%. These instruments combine government-rate duration, carry and credit exposure, so issuer execution and restructuring terms gave the more useful view of access. The broad message was functioning funding with pronounced differentiation across business quality, collateral and capital-structure position.

The scale of new financing is becoming a duration issue in its own right. US investment-grade sales reached $1 trillion at the fastest pace since 2020, driven partly by AI investment. Apollo President Jim Zelter said investment-grade issuance could exceed net Treasury sales during the year and cited roughly $1.4 trillion of new Treasury cash financing in the fiscal year. His forecast is not a completed flow outcome, though it captures the competition for long-dated capital across technology, utilities, defense and government borrowing.

Schneider Electric illustrated how strong equity performance can widen financing options. The company launched an €800 million convertible due 2034, with a proposed 0.25%-0.75% coupon and a conversion premium of 35%-40%. It planned to use €650 million to repurchase a convertible due in November 2030 and the balance for general purposes. Schneider’s share price had risen above the old bond’s conversion price, making refinancing a way to address dilution risk while offering investors continued exposure to data-center power and cooling demand.

Distressed credit showed a much harsher form of liability management. Apollo, Reno de Medici and holders of the packaging company’s €600 million bonds reached a preliminary agreement under which bondholders would convert part of their claims into majority equity and provide new funding. Negotiations still had to incorporate lenders to a €145 million revolving facility. With the bonds quoted near 26 cents on the euro and free cash flow expected to remain negative through 2026, control transfer and senior liquidity were replacing conventional refinancing.

The municipal market supplied another idiosyncratic loss case. A Nassau County agency failed to make a $36 million principal payment on June 1, the 1st default in the roughly $80 billion tobacco-settlement bond sector. The securities depend on payments linked to cigarette sales, and distributions to participating states and territories fell 19% in 2025. Bonds maturing in 2046 traded at 58.5 cents on June 2. The structure extends unpaid principal against future settlement revenue, leaving recovery tied to a declining cash-flow base.

Together, the cases define a segmented market. Investment-grade and equity-linked borrowers can use favorable valuations to refinance and fund expansion, while weak operating cash flow forces creditors into ownership and new-money support. Positive listed returns and clean Treasury cash operations showed no broad withdrawal of liquidity. They coexisted with security-specific impairment and with a rising volume of public and corporate duration that may require greater compensation over time.

## Global Duration, Central Banks, And Fiscal Transmission

European growth data offered duration support ahead of a still-difficult inflation debate. Euro-area retail volume fell 0.4% in April after a revised 0.8% March gain, slightly weaker than the 0.3% decline expected. Sales were 1.0% higher from a year earlier, but non-food volumes fell 0.9% on the month and automotive fuel dropped 2.7%. The release pointed to restrained goods demand, limiting the case that every energy-driven price increase can be met with a sustained policy tightening cycle.

Sweden showed why national curves need not move in lockstep. Core CPIF inflation rose to 0.5% in May from 0%, above the 0.3% survey median but well below the Riksbank’s 0.9% projection. Headline CPIF increased to 1.5% from 0.8%, close to the central bank’s forecast, and the krona strengthened modestly. Fiscal relief on food and fuel has muted pass-through, allowing the 1.75% policy rate to remain a credible near-term anchor even as markets preserve some tightening premium.

Czech inflation also moderated the regional hawkish impulse. Headline prices rose 2.1% from a year earlier in May, below the 2.3% median estimate and in line with the central bank’s projection. Services inflation slowed to 4.7%, still elevated enough to keep second-round effects in focus. With the policy rate at 3.5%, the mix weakens the case for an immediate increase while preserving sensitivity to any renewed energy pass-through into underlying prices.

New Zealand demonstrated the power of supply scarcity independently of policy expectations. Bids totaled NZ$1.4 billion for NZ$225 million of 2031 government bonds, producing a record 6.22 bid-to-cover ratio for the security after the government reduced projected borrowing. The benchmark 5-year yield had fallen more than 5 basis points since the budget, even as investors continued to price eventual Reserve Bank tightening. Reduced issuance and high absolute carry drew demand, separating sovereign-supply performance from the policy-rate outlook.

India remained the clearest developed-to-emerging transmission channel from energy and currency pressure. The rupee was down 6% for the year and had approached 97 per dollar, even as sovereign yields stayed broadly stable during the quarter. People familiar with Reliance Industries’ treasury discussions described possible shifts toward shorter money-market paper and less long-duration exposure; the company denied expressing a formal rate or currency view. The episode still illustrates how oil-import costs and currency defense can shorten private demand for duration before sovereign funding itself becomes disorderly.

## EM Sovereigns, Dollar Liquidity, And Debt-Management Risk

Emerging-market debt participated in the calmer close, with EMB gaining 0.2085% as the dollar softened. The broad return concealed sharply different sovereign mechanisms. Commodity exposure and yield supported the Democratic Republic of Congo, contractual thresholds dominated Zambia, and additional borrowing challenged Chile’s fiscal credibility. External liquidity was available, though its price depended on each borrower’s revenue base and debt-management choices.

Congo’s 2032 and 2037 dollar bonds rose for a 3rd session and remained above par, yielding 8.3% and 9.03%. Investors looked through an Ebola outbreak concentrated in a conflict-affected region and focused on copper and cobalt exposure to power, electrification and AI infrastructure. The sovereign had raised $1.25 billion in its April market debut. High carry and strategic mineral revenue supported the debt, while any disruption to production or exports remains the channel through which the health crisis could reach sovereign cash flow.

Zambia’s 2053 bond remained a negotiation over recovery value and contract mechanics. Morgan Stanley expected the government might raise its early buyback price to about 85 cents from 78 cents, while the bond traded at 82.14 cents. Acceptance near 75% is needed to activate a clean-up call, yet a blocking group controls more than 25% of the notes. The incentive to retire the security comes from a contingent coupon increase to 7.5% from 0.5% if IMF debt-sustainability conditions are met. A higher offer may improve participation while reducing the government’s economic benefit.

Chile faced a conventional supply problem from a stronger credit position. The government sought authority to issue another $6.2 billion on top of $17.4 billion already permitted for 2026. Peso bonds due 2032 cleared 12 basis points above secondary-market yields, and a later 2056 sale required a 10-basis-point concession. Gross debt was 42.6% of GDP in March and is projected to exceed the government’s 45% prudence threshold by 2028. Chile retains the highest sovereign ratings in Latin America, but larger local supply or additional dollar borrowing would transmit fiscal slippage into yields and spreads.

The dispersion argues against treating the positive fund return as uniform credit improvement. Congo is being rewarded for scarce commodity exposure and a real-money buyer base. Zambia’s outcome depends on participation, tender economics and a step-up clause. Chile’s issue is whether fiscal consolidation accompanies added market funding. Dollar softness eased the common external constraint for the day, leaving country-specific cash flows, institutions and liability terms to determine marginal pricing.

## Cross asset

Crude delivered the strongest cross-asset support for the Treasury rally. WTI fell 3.1035% to $93.04 and Brent declined 2.8422% to $95.03. The retreat aligned with the 2-basis-point drop in 10-year breakevens and helped reverse the previous session’s inflation impulse. Oil remained high enough to sustain concern about goods, transport and fertilizer costs, so the move reduced near-term pressure without resolving the broader pass-through risk.

Equities and volatility rejected a recessionary interpretation of lower yields. The S&P 500 rose 0.4055%, while the Nasdaq 100 fell 0.5346% and the semiconductor index dropped 2.1518%. The VIX declined 4.1096% to 15.4. Broad risk appetite held, but the weakness in AI-sensitive growth shares complicated the capital-spending narrative that has supported corporate issuance and real demand. The rates rally therefore occurred alongside sector rotation, not a uniform flight to safety.

The dollar index slipped 0.1206% to 99.41, easing the external financing backdrop for emerging borrowers. Gold gained 0.8813% to $4,475.7998, preserving some demand for geopolitical and inflation protection even as volatility fell. Positive credit-fund returns completed an orderly configuration: lower oil and a softer dollar reinforced nominal Treasury gains, while firm gold, sticky real yields and mixed technology equities kept the regime from looking fully normalized.

## Forward Market Significance

The May employment report at 08:30 ET on June 5, followed by May CPI at 08:30 ET on June 10 and PPI plus weekly claims at 08:30 ET on June 11, will determine whether June 4’s breakeven-led rally can broaden into lower real yields. Payroll growth near the 85,000 consensus, unemployment holding at 4.3% and contained wage breadth would leave the current interpretation intact: labor is cooling gradually, yet not enough to erase the Fed’s tightening option. A materially weaker employment mix followed by softer core inflation would challenge the restrictive real-rate floor and pull the 2- and 5-year sectors lower. Strong hiring or broader core and pipeline inflation would reopen June 3’s selloff, with front-end futures, 5- to 10-year real yields and breakevens responding through different channels.

The Reserve Bank of India decision on June 5 and the European Central Bank decision on June 11 provide the global policy test as energy-importing economies assess inflation against weaker demand. A steady 5.25% RBI rate accompanied by measures that stabilize the rupee, together with ECB guidance limiting the persistence of any increase, would confirm that national reaction functions can remain differentiated as crude retreats. Renewed oil gains, weaker importer currencies or guidance emphasizing second-round price pressure would challenge that relief and lift local front ends, global term-premium competition and the dollar funding burden. Swedish and Czech inflation showed that pass-through is not synchronized; the next policy language will reveal whether that dispersion survives another change in energy prices.

Treasury’s June 8-9 bill auctions and June 9-11 coupon sequence will test whether the clean short-end absorption seen on June 4 extends into duration. The $89 billion 13-week and $77 billion 26-week sales settle June 11, alongside $65 billion of 6-week and $50 billion of 52-week supply; the $58 billion 3-year, $39 billion 10-year and $22 billion 30-year issues settle June 15. Strong cover, broad direct and indirect participation, limited dealer takedown and stable front-end yields would confirm that macro repricing remains separate from funding stress. Weak coupon demand after payrolls and CPI would challenge that view, steepen the curve through supply concession and increase the chance that mortgage-convexity hedging amplifies the move as corporate borrowers compete for the same duration capacity.

section_summaries.market_read: The rally eased nominal pressure without dislodging the restrictive real-rate regime.
section_summaries.next_check: Whether softer macro data can finally pull real yields lower remains the decisive test.
section_summaries.summary: The June 4 close reversed part of the prior selloff through lower inflation compensation, while real yields stayed elevated. Orderly financing conditions helped contain the move, but Fed optionality, incoming public and corporate supply, and mortgage hedging risk left it as conditional relief rather than a duration breakout.

section_summaries.market_read: Policy repricing stayed distinct from funding stress, but duration supply remains untested.
section_summaries.next_check: Coupon-auction participation and real-yield behavior after payrolls will test the split.
section_summaries.section_title: UST Rates, Fed Pricing, Real Yields, And Curve Structure
section_summaries.summary: 2- and 5-year yields led a modest rally and slight bull steepening, but the benchmark move came entirely through lower breakevens as the 10-year real yield held firm. Strong bill demand showed no cash squeeze, shifting the next risk toward coupon absorption and mortgage hedging.

section_summaries.market_read: Orderly aggregate returns mask rising dispersion in access, recovery value and structure.
section_summaries.next_check: New-issue concessions and distressed financing terms will show whether selectivity deepens.
section_summaries.summary: Positive credit-fund returns and broad investment-grade access indicated functioning liquidity, yet financing remained sharply selective. Equity-linked issuers could refinance and fund expansion, while distressed borrowers faced ownership transfer, new-money support or payment impairment as public and corporate duration supply competed for capital.

section_summaries.market_read: Global duration remained driven by local supply and pass-through, not a synchronized policy turn.
section_summaries.next_check: Central-bank guidance will show whether policy dispersion survives renewed energy pressure.
section_summaries.section_title: Global Duration, Central Banks, And Fiscal Transmission
section_summaries.summary: Weaker euro-area demand and softer Czech inflation supported duration, while Sweden’s inflation mix preserved national policy divergence. New Zealand’s scarce supply drew exceptional demand despite tightening expectations, whereas India showed how oil and currency pressure can shorten private duration appetite before sovereign funding destabilizes.

section_summaries.market_read: Dollar relief eased the common constraint while liability terms and fiscal choices drove dispersion.
section_summaries.next_check: Commodity cash flows, Zambia’s participation threshold and Chile’s funding concessions remain decisive.
section_summaries.section_title: EM Sovereigns, Dollar Liquidity, And Debt-Management Risk
section_summaries.summary: A softer dollar helped emerging-market debt, but sovereign pricing remained country-specific rather than uniformly stronger. Congo benefited from strategic-mineral cash flows, Zambia hinged on tender participation and contractual economics, and Chile faced a supply concession as added borrowing tested fiscal credibility.

section_summaries.market_read: Cross-asset pricing framed the rates move as orderly inflation relief, not a growth scare.
section_summaries.next_check: Oil pass-through and AI-sensitive equity performance will test whether that balance persists.
section_summaries.summary: Falling crude and a softer dollar reinforced the nominal Treasury rally and eased external financing conditions. Rising broad equities and lower volatility rejected a recessionary signal, but technology weakness, firm gold and sticky real yields kept the cross-asset regime from looking fully normalized.

section_summaries.market_read: The next move depends on whether macro relief and duration absorption arrive together.
section_summaries.next_check: Payrolls, inflation, policy guidance and coupon demand must confirm the same easing thread.
section_summaries.summary: 3 tests will determine whether the breakeven-led rally broadens: US labor and inflation data, RBI and ECB policy guidance, and the Treasury auction sequence. Softer data, contained energy pass-through and strong coupon demand would extend relief; opposite outcomes would pressure real yields, front ends and curve supply.

methodology_note: Market levels are shown on an NY-close basis unless otherwise noted.

bullets: 10Y TIPS real yield 2.11% (0 bp) | 10Y breakeven inflation 2.36% (-2 bp)
UST 2s10s curve 42 bp (+1 bp) | UST 2s30s curve 92 bp (+1 bp)
overall_summary.headline: Lower oil and softer labor signals drove a modest Treasury rally, but real yields stayed firm
overall_summary.session_narrative: Treasury yields fell modestly, led by the front end, as higher claims, lower revised unit labor costs and retreating crude reduced near-term inflation pressure. The benchmark nominal decline came through lower breakevens rather than easier real yields, while credit returns and bill demand remained orderly. Firm policy optionality, a large coupon calendar and mortgage-convexity risk limited the move.
overall_summary.summary: Front-end outperformance and lower inflation compensation produced a small bull steepening at the June 4 NY close. Softer labor-cost pressure and retreating crude eased the immediate inflation impulse, while sticky real yields and looming duration supply kept the restrictive-rate regime intact.

watch_events.market_implication: Soft data would pull 2- and 5-year yields lower; strength would revive real yields and breakevens.
watch_events.market_trigger: Material labor weakness with softer core inflation, or strong hiring with broad inflation.
watch_events.title: US labor and inflation sequence
watch_events.watch_line: Test payrolls near 85,000, 4.3% unemployment and contained wages against inflation breadth.

watch_events.market_implication: Local front ends, global term premium and the dollar funding burden would rise.
watch_events.market_trigger: Renewed oil gains, weaker importer currencies or emphasis on second-round prices.
watch_events.title: RBI and ECB policy test
watch_events.watch_line: Watch rupee support, ECB inflation-persistence guidance and renewed energy pass-through.

watch_events.market_implication: Supply concessions would steepen the curve and raise mortgage-hedging amplification risk.
watch_events.market_trigger: Weak coupon demand after payrolls and CPI, especially with heavier dealer absorption.
watch_events.title: Treasury duration absorption test
watch_events.watch_line: Track cover, bidder breadth, dealer takedown and front-end stability across the sequence.
